concepts.name: Graph
concepts.short_definition: A collection of nodes (vertices) connected by edges.

concepts.name: Vertex
concepts.short_definition: A fundamental unit in a graph, representing an entity.

concepts.name: Edge
concepts.short_definition: A link between two vertices in a graph.

concepts.name: Directed Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges have a direction.

concepts.name: Undirected Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges do not have a direction.

concepts.name: Adjacency List
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph where each vertex has a list of its adjacent vertices.

concepts.name: Adjacency Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph using a matrix where entries indicate the presence of an edge.

concepts.name: Traversal
concepts.short_definition: The process of visiting each vertex in a graph exactly once.

concepts.name: Depth-First Search
concepts.short_definition: A graph traversal algorithm that explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking.

concepts.name: Recursion
concepts.short_definition: A programming technique where a function calls itself.

concepts.name: Stack
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that follows the Last-In, First-Out (LIFO) principle.

concepts.name: Visited Set
concepts.short_definition: A collection to keep track of vertices that have already been visited during traversal.

concepts.name: DFS Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: The step-by-step process of performing a Depth-First Search.

concepts.name: DFS Recursive Implementation
concepts.short_definition: Implementing DFS using recursive function calls.

concepts.name: DFS Iterative Implementation
concepts.short_definition: Implementing DFS using an explicit stack data structure.

concepts.name: Starting Vertex
concepts.short_definition: The vertex from which the DFS traversal begins.

concepts.name: Backtracking
concepts.short_definition: Returning to a previous state or vertex after exploring a path.

concepts.name: Tree
concepts.short_definition: A hierarchical data structure that is a special case of a graph.

concepts.name: DFS Tree
concepts.short_definition: The tree formed by the edges traversed during a DFS.

concepts.name: Discovery Time
concepts.short_definition: The time at which a vertex is first visited during DFS.

concepts.name: Finish Time
concepts.short_definition: The time at which a vertex and all its descendants have been fully explored.

concepts.name: Parent Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer from a vertex to the vertex from which it was discovered.

concepts.name: Cycle Detection
concepts.short_definition: Identifying if a graph contains any cycles using DFS.

concepts.name: Topological Sort
concepts.short_definition: Linear ordering of vertices in a directed acyclic graph (DAG) such that for every directed edge uv, vertex u comes before v in the ordering.

concepts.name: Strongly Connected Components
concepts.short_definition: Maximal subgraphs of a directed graph such that for every pair of vertices u and v in the subgraph, there is a directed path from u to v and a directed path from v to u.

concepts.name: Connected Components
concepts.short_definition: Maximal subgraphs of an undirected graph such that for every pair of vertices u and v in the subgraph, there is a path between u and v.

concepts.name: Graph Representation
concepts.short_definition: Methods for storing graph data, such as adjacency lists or matrices.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the computational resources (time) required by an algorithm.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the computational resources (memory) required by an algorithm.

concepts.name: Path Finding
concepts.short_definition: Finding a sequence of vertices connecting two given vertices.

concepts.name: Graph Coloring
concepts.short_definition: Assigning colors to vertices such that no two adjacent vertices share the same color.

concepts.name: Articulation Point
concepts.short_definition: A vertex whose removal increases the number of connected components in a graph.

concepts.name: Bridge
concepts.short_definition: An edge whose removal increases the number of connected components in a graph.

concepts.name: Bipartite Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph whose vertices can be divided into two disjoint and independent sets.

concepts.name: Graph Algorithms
concepts.short_definition: Algorithms designed to operate on graph data structures.

concepts.name: Algorithm Analysis
concepts.short_definition: The process of evaluating the efficiency of an algorithm.

concepts.name: Data Structures
concepts.short_definition: Ways of organizing and storing data.

concepts.name: Abstract Data Types
concepts.short_definition: A mathematical model for data structures.

concepts.name: Computational Thinking
concepts.short_definition: A problem-solving approach involving decomposition, pattern recognition, abstraction, and algorithms.
